_iteration_doc: Iter-2 supersedes iter-1 after QA report ba-qa-report-20260525-095242.json FAIL verdict (5 critical + 1 major + 1 minor blockers B1-B7). Codex iter-2 review surfaced 8 additional findings (F1-F8) all applied. Matrix grew 16 → 33 case rows (28 primary + 5 -supp regression). All baselines re-verified empirically against current hook before re-emission. See docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260525-095242.json revision_metadata for the full delta.
_risk_level_doc: Medium because (a) the change is in a PreToolUse safety hook (defensive layer of the user-gated bulk-commit auth-flag contract); (b) impact is local — single elif branch in Layer 1.F of hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh plus test extension; (c) no UI, no schema migration, no public API change. Triggers test-writer per dev.md Step 8 (complexity_tier >= STANDARD).
_test_writer_expected_doc: STANDARD tier + security-fix-to-hook predicate. test-writer should ingest acceptance-criteria-20260525-095242.json AC-01 (16-case matrix) and AC-02 (pytest extension contract).

requirement.original: 修复 hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh 复合命令绕过 — Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path 仍然 ALLOW; compound `ls <flag> ; <write>` / `&&` / `||` / `|` / `$(...)` / backtick + write 必须 DENY (exit 2). 扩展或新建 test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py 覆盖复合命令旁路。
requirement.clarified: Tighten hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F (lines 633-671) allowlist branch (b) from PREFIX-read semantics to WHOLE-COMMAND-read semantics: when the command mentions a protected glob (/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag or /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json), ALLOW only when the WHOLE command is a single pure-read invocation with NO shell control structure / NO recursive shell / NO variable assignment / NO redirect. Also tighten allowlist branch (a) so only the BARE scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py invocation form (no separators) is allowed. Extend tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py with a 16-case parametrized matrix covering separators ; && || | , command substitution $(), backtick ``, process substitution <(), variable assignment, recursive shell bash -c / python -c, official-writer compound, plus negative controls.
requirement.what: Surgical regex predicate tightening in Layer 1.F of hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh + parametrized test extension in tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py.
requirement.why: Prior cycle 20260524-205206 AC-04 landed Layer 1.F as the hook-layer floor of the M4 user-gated bulk-commit auth-flag contract, but QA + Codex reproduced a compound-command bypass: `ls <flag> ; python3 -c <write>` returns hook exit 0 because the allowlist regex matches the prefix and the redirect-veto regex only checks > / >> / tee — defeating the hook layer of a 3-layer defense (script gate + commit.md doc + hook deny). The hook is the only layer the user explicitly named as the floor; the bypass invalidates that floor.

included: Replace Layer 1.F allowlist branch (b) prefix-read predicate with whole-command-read predicate (no shell control structure, no recursive shell, no redirect, no variable assignment, no `-c`/`-e`/`eval`/`source`).
Tighten allowlist branch (a) — only the BARE python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py invocation form passes; compound shapes including the script are denied.
Optional: refactor the predicate into a helper function for readability (behavior-equivalent).
Update inline comment block lines 633-647 to reflect new whole-command semantics (S1).

Extend tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py (or add a sibling test file) with the AC-01 16-case parametrized matrix, asserting exact exit code AND presence/absence of the stable stderr token `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write`.
excluded: Defense against base64-encoded or otherwise obfuscated writes where the literal protected path does NOT appear in the command text (codex finding #7 OBSERVATION_ONLY; explicitly Won't Have).
Reworking allowlist branch (b) to require a pre-grant for read inspection (codex finding #2 OBSERVATION_ONLY; user's AC explicitly requires pure-read ALLOW to remain).
Touching hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh, scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, commands/commit.md operational logic, hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh — none on the bypass path.
Re-running prior cycle's AC-01/02/03/05 — already PASS per qa-report-20260524-205206.json iter-3.
Widening the protected-path glob beyond /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag and /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json.
Adding deny patterns for the literal substring `--bulk` or `claude-bulk` in unrelated paths (would block specs/reports/tests).

success_criteria: AC-01: all 33 case rows in the iter-2 bypass matrix produce the expected exit code + stderr token. Breakdown: 9 ALLOW (5 pure-read incl. C3b cat-flag + C21 bare-find; 3 negative-control lookalike; 1 bare official-writer) + 24 DENY (15 real bypass-exhibitors that go from rc=0 today → rc=2 after fix; 6 regression guards already rc=2 today; 3 M2 official-writer-safelist tightening).

AC-02: `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260524-205206/ -q` exits 0 with all 33 new parametrized rows passing AND all 21 prior-cycle directory-level cases still passing (per QA observation O1 — 21 is the DIRECTORY-LEVEL pytest --collect-only count, not test_AC_04_* file count).
Layer 1.F comment block accurately documents the new whole-command-read semantics.
constraints: Behavior-only contract: AC validates exit code + stderr token, not source form. Dev may use bash regex stack OR a small Python helper.
No widening of the protected glob; no broad substring blocks.
Hook must remain self-contained (no new external dependencies).

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh (reviewed — guards tool-input file_path channel; not on the Bash-compound-bypass path)
scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py (reviewed — enforces in-process auth-flag check; not on the Bash-compound-bypass path)
hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh (reviewed — session-Stop cleanup; not on the bypass path)
commands/commit.md (reviewed — documentation-as-policy at Step 1; not on the bypass path; C1 nice-to-have only)

component_chain.role: Layer 1.F — protected-glob mention deny-by-default + allowlist branches (a) bare official-writer invocation and (b) pure-read inspection
component_chain.imports_from: leaf (self-contained bash hook reading JSON payload from stdin)
component_chain.evidence: Read hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 633-671; verified the allowlist branch (b) at lines 651-654 uses prefix-anchored regex `(^|[[:space:];|&])(ls|stat|cat|...)` and only vetoes on redirect tokens (>|>>|tee) — does not split on shell separators or assert whole-command shape.

component_chain.role: Parametrized pytest harness with _run_hook_with_payload helper (lines 54-63) and existing @pytest.mark.parametrize block (lines 99-126) covering prior cycle's g_hook_1..8 cases. Extension surface for AC-01's 16-case matrix.
component_chain.imports_from: subprocess (stdlib) — invokes hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh with mocked JSON payloads via stdin
component_chain.evidence: Read confirms existing _run_hook_with_payload helper and parametrized block layout; AC-02 specifies either extending this block in-place or adding a sibling test file with the same helper invocation pattern.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Layer 1.F entry gate — only invokes the allowlist/deny logic when the command mentions one of the two protected globs.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Existing allowlist branch (b) — prefix-read allow: grep -qE '(^|[[:space:];|&])(ls|stat|cat|file|wc|head|tail|grep|jq|find|test|\[)\s+[^>]*/tmp/claude-bulk-' && ! grep -qE '(>|>>|tee\b)'
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Allowlist read-only inspection of protected globs. CURRENT: too permissive (prefix-match). This cycle TIGHTENS the predicate; the allowlist SEMANTICS are preserved (pure-read still allowed), only the PREDICATE is narrowed.
pre_existing_guards._removal_authorized_note: Authorized for narrowing only — the allowlist BEHAVIOR (pure-read still allowed) must be preserved per user's verbatim AC. The PREDICATE is replaced; the BRANCH is not removed.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Existing allowlist branch (a) — bare official-writer allow: grep -qE 'scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py'
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Allowlist the BARE invocation of the official sentinel writer. CURRENT: matches substring anywhere — too permissive (a compound including the script + a side-write slips through). This cycle TIGHTENS via M2 — same no-shell-control-structure predicate applies.
pre_existing_guards._removal_authorized_note: Authorized for narrowing only — branch (a) BEHAVIOR (bare invocation allowed) preserved; PREDICATE narrowed.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Existing deny branch — fall-through deny with stderr token `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write`
pre_existing_guards.purpose: The stable stderr token QA tests grep for. MUST be preserved verbatim (or extended) — AC-01/AC-02 assert this exact token presence.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: `ls /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag ; python3 -c 'open("/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag","w").write("y")'` returns hook exit 0 — the auth flag is created without user authorization, defeating the M4 user-gated bulk-disable contract.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Layer 1.F allowlist branch (b) at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:651-654 uses a prefix-anchored regex `(^|[[:space:];|&])(ls|stat|cat|...)` that matches whenever any read-only verb appears anywhere preceded by a shell separator — the regex treats the prefix as the entire command. The redirect-veto regex `(>|>>|tee\b)` only catches a narrow set of write tokens, missing `python -c`, `node -e`, `perl -e`, `bash -c`, eval, source, here-docs, process substitution, etc. Result: a compound command whose first segment is `ls <flag>` and whose later segment writes via any non-redirect channel slips through the allowlist.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: Prior cycle commit landed via ticket-20260524-205206 (M4.3 hook deny implementation; the allowlist branches were added in the same cycle).
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Cycle 5 of the parent ticket added the read-only allowlist branch (b) to satisfy the user's verbatim requirement that pure `ls/stat/cat <flag>` for diagnostic purposes still works. The implementer used a prefix-anchored regex assuming the regex's `(^|[[:space:];|&])` boundary plus the redirect-veto would be sufficient.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The prefix anchor treats the regex match as the entire command. Compound commands where the prefix is the read verb but the suffix is a write (via any non-redirect channel) bypass the veto. Codex finding #1 calls this the 'split-and-reparse' anti-pattern — relying on regex over compound text instead of asserting whole-command shape.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced in prior cycle 20260524-205206 M4.3 implementation. QA reproduced the bypass in close-cycle iter-3 review. User confirmed and dispatched this cycle.
observations: Bypass with separator `;`: hook exit 0 (observed: BA reproduced via subprocess invocation against current hook with mocked JSON payload)
Bypass with separator `&&`: hook exit 0 (observed: same method)
Bypass with separator `||`: hook exit 0 (observed: same method)
Bypass with separator `|`: hook exit 0 (observed: same method)
Pure `ls /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag` (no separator): hook exit 0 (observed: correct — intentional pure-read allow)
Pure `python3 -c 'open(...).write("y")'` (no `ls` prefix, path inside python source): hook exit 2 (observed: current write-only path is already caught)
Root location: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:648-671 (inferred from Read of hook source)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: BA invoked hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh via `printf '%s' '<json>' | bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; echo exit=$?` with each of the 4 separator-bypass payloads and the 2 control payloads. Observed exit 0 for all 4 bypass cases and the pure-read control; exit 2 for the pure write control. Walked back to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:633-671 via Read; identified allowlist branch (b) at lines 651-654 as the culprit (prefix-anchored regex + redirect-only veto).

git_bisect_result._triggered_note: Not a regression — this is a defect in a feature landed in the immediately-prior cycle (20260524-205206). The introducing commit is known (the M4.3 implementation commit of the parent ticket). Bisect would be tautological.

_diagnosis_layer_doc: L4 — hook logic / regex predicates. The defect is in the conditional structure (regex predicate is too liberal); the fix tightens the same predicate in the same layer. Not L3 (data values are preserved — the verb allowlist `ls|stat|cat|...` and the protected globs are unchanged) and not L5 (no infrastructure / build change).

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 修复 hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh 复合命令绕过
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC-01 — Layer 1.F whole-command-read predicate denies all compound bypass shapes (16-case matrix) while preserving pure-read ALLOW.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Test coverage: extend ... test_AC_04_... with compound-command bypass cases. At minimum 4-6 new cases covering each separator type.
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC-02 — parametrized pytest extension covering AC-01's 16-case matrix using existing _run_hook_with_payload helper; exact rc + stable stderr token contract; no regression on prior 21 cases.

regression_investigation_checklist._triggered_note: Not a regression bug in the global-CSS / middleware sense. This is a defect in a feature landed in the immediately-prior cycle; the introducing change is well-localized. Bisect / globals.css / middleware checks not applicable.

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh is a PreToolUse safety hook executed by the Claude Code harness on stdin JSON payloads; verification is via subprocess invocation with mocked payloads in pytest. Test extension target is tests/generated/ — not a user-facing surface.

evidence.observed: 复合命令绕过 — `ls /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag ; python3 -c 'open("/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag","w").write("y")'` returns hook exit 0

measured.value: exit 0 for compound bypass with separators `;`, `&&`, `||`, `|` against current hook

expected.value: exit 2 + stderr containing `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write` for all compound shapes; exit 0 only for single bare pure-read invocations on the protected glob
expected.source: User dispatch verbatim AC matrix + Codex review finding #1 (anchored single-command predicate)

evidence.gap: Layer 1.F currently allows ANY compound command whose first segment is a read verb on the protected path; required behavior is to allow ONLY a single pure-read with no shell control structure / no recursive shell / no redirect / no variable assignment.

reference_source.description: Verbatim user requirement (this cycle dispatch); BA's own reproduction of the bypass against the current hook source; Codex adversarial review of the fix approach (9 findings: 7 applied + 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY).
reference_source.url_or_location: User dispatch prompt; hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:633-671 (Read this cycle); /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-2720452-1779702937.txt (codex output).

attempts.proposed_solution: Hook deny based on path-mention pattern catching python3 -c / node -e / perl -e / here-docs / base64; allowlist branch (b) for read-only inspection (ls|stat|cat|...) prefix-match + redirect-only veto.
attempts.actual_change: Layer 1.F at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:633-671 — wraps protected-glob mention with deny-by-default + 2 allowlist branches (a) bare script + (b) read-only prefix.
attempts.outcome: Allowlist branch (b) is too permissive — compound command whose first segment is `ls <flag>` and later segment writes via `python -c` slips through. QA + Codex Cycle-5 reproduced the bypass for separators `;`, `&&`, `||`, `|`.

attempts.proposed_solution: Whole-command-read predicate stack in Layer 1.F branch (b); bare-invocation predicate in branch (a); 16-case AC matrix.
attempts.actual_change: Iter-1 BA artifacts only — no Dev fix landed yet.
attempts.outcome: QA FAIL (5 critical + 1 major + 1 minor): AC matrix had spec_text_vs_execution_drift — C7/C8/C9 were labeled as Layer 1.F prefix-allowlist bypass verifiers but were already rc=2 today via OTHER layers (tee-veto, deny-by-default on echo-prefix). C3b (cat-flag user-verbatim ALLOW) was missing. C15 negative control had no `claude-bulk` substring so entry-gate never fired. M1 enumerated xargs/find -exec/-delete but matrix had ZERO cases. Codex iter-2 found 8 additional gaps (newline/&/|&/if-then separator coverage, bare-find ALLOW, sentinel-glob lookalike, M2 safelist spoof).
attempts._failure_detail: The semantic fix direction was correct (predicate-tightening at L4) but the AC matrix did not actually exercise the bypass surface it claimed to. This is a BA-spec defect, not a Dev-implementation defect.

novelty_check.rationale: Iter-2 differs from prior-cycle (20260524-205206) by inverting predicate semantics (prefix-read → whole-command-read). Iter-2 differs from iter-1 by: (a) replacing C7/C8/C9 payloads with REAL prefix-allowlist bypass exhibitors that QA empirically verified rc=0 today; (b) adding C3b cat-flag user-verbatim ALLOW (B4); (c) adding C15/C15b lookalike entry-gate guards (B5+F1+F2); (d) adding C17/C18/C19 for M1's xargs/find-exec/find-delete enumeration (B6); (e) adding C20 compound-no-write semantics lock (B7); (f) adding C22-C25 for M1's newline/&/|&/if-then separator coverage (codex F4); (g) adding C21 bare-find ALLOW (codex F5); (h) adding C26/C27 for M2 safelist spoof coverage (codex F7); (i) recategorizing C11/C12/C13 as regression guards (codex F3); (j) recategorizing C14 as M2 (codex F6); (k) clarifying the directory-level 21-case count (QA O1). Every single change is empirically grounded in QA's verified rc baseline or codex's verified bypass shape. No proposed change is speculative; all are runtime-measured. This is a fundamentally different matrix that exercises the actual Layer 1.F bypass surface.

context.codebase_state: modified (.claude/* working tree) — see git status pre-dispatch; no pending edits to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh or tests/generated/20260524-205206/
context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification; prior cycle 20260524-205206 landed the M4 user-gated bulk-commit contract (3 layers).

development_approach.strategy: Surgical regex predicate tightening. Replace Layer 1.F allowlist branch (b) with a stacked-predicate check that asserts the WHOLE command is a single pure-read (no shell control structure / no recursive shell / no redirect / no variable assignment). Tighten branch (a) with the same no-shell-control assertion. Optionally factor predicates into a helper function. Update inline comment block to document new semantics. Extend pytest with the 16-case parametrized matrix using existing _run_hook_with_payload helper.
development_approach.validation_approach: AC-01: subprocess-invoke the hook with each of the 16 mocked JSON payloads; assert exit code + stderr token presence/absence. AC-02: run `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260524-205206/ -q` and assert all new cases pass + all 21 prior cases still pass. Cross-cut: dev MAY refactor the predicate into a helper function; QA only validates BEHAVIOR (exit + stderr), not source form.

_iter2_rerun.feedback_summary: Codex iter-2 (gpt-5.5 xhigh) was re-invoked after QA FAIL on iter-1 to adversarially review the proposed iter-2 matrix. Codex returned 8 findings (F1-F8): F1 (C15 lookalike too weak — needs touch-then-ls compound), F2 (sentinel-glob lookalike missing), F3 (C11/C12/C13 are regression guards, not bypass exhibitors — relabel), F4 (newline/&/|&/if-then separator coverage missing — M1 enumerates them), F5 (bare-find ALLOW per M1 missing from AC), F6 (C14 should be re-categorized as M2 not prefix-allowlist), F7 (M2 safelist spoof — comment-spoof + no-py-prefix forms), F8 (matrix case-count consistency). All 8 findings empirically verified by BA against current hook and applied to iter-2 matrix. No OBSERVATION_ONLY findings this round.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex iter-1 (gpt-5.5 xhigh) issued 9 findings against the draft fix approach. 7 are PROPOSED_FIX (all applied to ticket M1/M2/AC-01); 2 are OBSERVATION_ONLY and out-of-scope per user AC. Output captured at /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-2720452-1779702937.txt during ticket drafting.

findings.issue: Split-and-reparse is fragile; use anchored single-command predicate
findings.proposed_fix: Stack of grep -qE predicates anchored on trimmed-command start, asserting NO shell control structure / NO recursive shell / NO redirect / NO assignment
findings.rationale: Cleanest approach; matches existing hook style; bash-native

findings.issue: Drop allowlist branch (b) entirely is safer
findings.rationale: User's verbatim AC requires pure-read ALLOW to remain — conflicts with the requirement

findings.proposed_fix: Tighten branch (a) to bare invocation only — same no-shell-control predicate
findings.rationale: M2 + AC-01 case C14 cover this
Codex itself classifies as out-of-scope; documented in Won't Have

findings.issue: Recursive shell deny even if inner is read-only
findings.proposed_fix: Deny -c / -e on bash|sh|zsh|dash|python|node|perl|ruby + eval + source + dot-source

findings.issue: Tests must assert exact rc == 2 and stable stderr token
findings.proposed_fix: Use assert rc == 2 + assert 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' in err (DENY) or rc == 0 + token absent (ALLOW)

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Iter-1: all 7 PROPOSED_FIX findings applied verbatim into ticket M1/M2 + AC-01 16-case matrix + AC-02 assertion shape. 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY findings logged as Won't Have (out-of-scope per user AC). Iter-2: all 8 codex findings (F1-F8) plus all 7 QA blockers (B1-B7) applied — matrix grew 16 → 33 case rows; added C3b (cat-flag user-verbatim ALLOW), C21 (bare-find ALLOW per M1), C15/C15b (lookalike entry-gate guards), C15c (retained iter-1 C15 as regression guard), C17/C18/C19 (xargs/find-exec/find-delete M1 verifiers), C20 (compound-no-write semantics lock), C22-C25 (newline/&/|&/if-then separator coverage), C26/C27 (M2 safelist spoof coverage), C7/C8/C9 replaced with real prefix-allowlist bypass-exhibitors + iter-1 forms retained as -supp regression guards, C11/C12/C13/C14 recategorized per codex F3/F6. Empirical baselines re-verified against current hook before re-emission.
